seoTitle: How to Have Two Outlook Accounts on One Computer
metaDescription: Learn how to run two Outlook accounts on one computer, either combined in a unified sidebar or as fully isolated profiles you switch between.
intro: Juggling a personal and professional inbox shouldn't mean constantly signing in and out or using separate devices. Outlook supports adding a second account to your existing sidebar, or setting up fully isolated profiles for stricter separation. This guide covers both approaches.

steps.heading: Open Account Info
steps.body: In your running Outlook app, click the File tab and look under Account Information.

steps.heading: Add a second account
steps.body: Click Add Account and type your secondary email address into the wizard.

steps.heading: Authenticate the new account
steps.body: Click Connect, enter your password, and complete any multi-factor authentication prompts.

steps.heading: Restart to see the new folders
steps.body: Click Done once setup finishes and restart Outlook to reveal the newly stacked folder layout.

steps.heading: Or set up an isolated profile
steps.body: For stricter separation, close Outlook, open Control Panel, find the classic Mail settings, and click Show Profiles.

steps.heading: Create a separate profile
steps.body: Click Add, name the profile, input your secondary credentials, and check Prompt for a profile to be used so you can pick which one loads on launch.

faq.question: What's the difference between adding an account and creating a profile?
faq.answer: Adding an account stacks a second inbox in the same unified sidebar, while a separate profile fully isolates folders and data behind a launch-time prompt.

faq.question: Will my two accounts' data ever mix together?
faq.answer: Each account uses its own local data file, so mail, contacts, and calendars stay separated even in the unified sidebar view.

faq.question: Can I set a different signature for each account?
faq.answer: Yes, you can assign dedicated send-from preferences and signatures per account to keep outgoing branding clean.

faq.question: Do I need to restart Outlook after adding an account?
faq.answer: Yes, restarting after setup ensures the new folder structure loads correctly in the sidebar.
